title: How an exoplanet odd couple survived by traveling in from the cold together
summary: The James Webb Space Telescope discovered that a mini-Neptune exoplanet formed far from its star and migrated inward, potentially explaining the origin of similar exoplanets. This finding sheds light on the atmospheric composition and migration history of such celestial bodies.

title: Europe's 1st reusable spacecraft 'Space Rider' clears key hurdles on the road to launch
summary: Europe's first reusable spacecraft, ESA's 'Space Rider,' has cleared key milestones in its development and qualification phases. The vehicle is now on track for its upcoming launch.

title: 'Whatever Russia is testing, it's sophisticated': 2 Russian satellites get within 10 feet of each other in orbit
summary: Two Russian satellites demonstrated advanced orbital maneuvering by approaching within 10 feet of each other. This proximity operation suggests sophisticated space capabilities.

title: 3 puzzles of our universe could be solved with this new dark matter theory
summary: A new dark matter theory that interacts with itself could solve three major cosmic puzzles. This theoretical approach offers potential explanations for long-standing mysteries in the universe.

title: 'Thor' hammered down 15 years ago, and it's still the only Marvel movie to do the God of Thunder justice
summary: The news discusses the Marvel movie 'Thor' released in 2011, highlighting its significance as the only film to do justice to the God of Thunder. It mentions the movie's impact and subsequent reception.

title: Sci-fi RPG 'Exodus''s combat looks and feels like 'Mass Effect', but the dialogue system could be a big upgrade (video)
summary: The article discusses the combat mechanics of the sci-fi RPG 'Exodus' compared to 'Mass Effect', highlighting its potential dialogue system as an upgrade. It mentions a community update featuring characters Jun Aslan and Phaedra.

title: Nikon Action 7x50 binoculars review
summary: Nikon has released a new pair of Action 7x50 binoculars aimed at astronomy novices. The review highlights their suitability for beginners in the field.

title: Why does NASA's Curiosity rover have a 'lucky penny' on Mars? | Space photo of the day for May 6, 2026
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover carries a 'lucky penny' on Mars, which is intended to provide good luck to the first person to find it. This tradition is part of a lighthearted approach to space exploration.

title: 10 hidden spots in Spain to see the rare sunset total solar eclipse on Aug. 12
summary: Spain will experience a rare sunset total solar eclipse on August 12, with hidden locations like desert badlands and hilltop villages offering optimal viewing. These off-the-beaten-track spots provide clear views of the celestial event.

title: Scientists created one of the largest simulations of our universe ever — about the size of 500,000 HD movies
summary: Scientists developed one of the largest universe simulations, FLAMINGO, to study galaxy, dark matter, and cosmic structure evolution over billions of years. The project's scale is equivalent to 500,000 HD movies.

title: Ames Science Stars of the Month May 2026
summary: NASA Ames Science Directorate recognized four individuals—Lora Jovanović, Tammy Moore, Frances Donovan, and Jaden Ta—as Stars of the Month for May 2026 for their outstanding contributions to the NASA mission.

title: NASA’s Dryden Aeronautical Test Range Supports Flight, Space Missions
summary: NASA's Dryden Aeronautical Test Range (DATR) supports flight and space missions by providing essential communications, tracking, and data services. The range operates under the Armstrong Flight Research Center in Edwards, California, enabling safe and effective research flights.

title: NASA Wallops to Host Public Information Session May 13
summary: NASA Wallops Flight Facility will host a public information session on May 13 to discuss activities including causeway bridge construction, beach replenishment, and updates from the GLOBE program. Federal and state health experts will also participate in the event.

title: NASA Sets Coverage for SpaceX 34th Station Resupply Launch, Arrival
summary: NASA and SpaceX are targeting May 12 for the 34th SpaceX resupply mission to the International Space Station, carrying 6,500 pounds of cargo. This marks another commercial resupply services mission under SpaceX's contract with NASA.

title: Unlocking the Mystery of X-ray Dots
summary: NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory discovered a new 'X-ray dot' that could help explain the nature of hundreds or thousands of similar objects. The finding follows reports of a new class of mysterious objects after the James Webb Space Telescope began its science observations.

title: NASA’s Roman Poised to Transform Hunt for Elusive Neutron Stars
summary: NASA's upcoming Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope may help detect elusive neutron stars, which are typically invisible despite being scattered throughout the Milky Way. A new study suggests the telescope's capabilities could reveal these remnants of exploded massive stars.

title: New NASA Technology Mimics Extreme Cold of the Lunar Night
summary: NASA is developing new technology to withstand the extreme cold of lunar nights, which can cause materials like rubber to shatter and electronics to fail. This research is crucial for future Moon and Mars exploration missions.

title: Melting Snow Off Shivelyuch
summary: Near-constant volcanic activity is observed at Shiveluch in Russia, causing melting snow. This geological event is ongoing and monitored closely.

title: NASA eClips and GLOBE Educators Strengthen a Regional STEM Ecosystem in Coastal Virginia
summary: NASA eClips and GLOBE educators partnered with Virginia school districts and community groups to enhance STEM education in the Tidewater region. The collaboration aims to strengthen regional STEM ecosystems through instructional practice improvements.

title: NASA’s Perseverance Mars Rover Surveys ‘Crocodile Bridge’
summary: NASA's Perseverance Mars rover captured a 360-degree panorama of a region called 'Crocodile Bridge' on Jezero Crater's rim using its Mastcam-Z camera system. The image is composed of 980 images taken on December 18, 2025.

title: Pad 1 conversion ongoing, with Flight 12 nearing
summary: Pad 1 is undergoing conversion after Flight 11, with Flight 12 approaching readiness. The modifications aim to support future missions.

title: Launch Preview: three launches — a calm before the storm?
summary: Three launches are scheduled, including two Falcon 9 missions and one Chang Zheng 7 mission, potentially marking a calm period before a busier launch phase.

title: SpaceX launches CAS500-2 and 45 other payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its third rideshare mission of 2026 aboard a Falcon 9 rocket, carrying CAS500-2 and 45 other payloads to a Sun-synchronous orbit.

title: SpaceX aims for mid-May Starship Flight 12 launch with revised trajectory
summary: SpaceX is targeting a mid-May launch for its twelfth Starship flight test, featuring a revised trajectory. The company is preparing for the return of Starship launches after recent adjustments.

title: Russia debuts new rocket with maiden Soyuz-5 launch
summary: Russia successfully conducted the maiden launch of its new Soyuz-5 rocket on Saturday. The debut flight marks a significant milestone for the country's space program.

title: Next NASA ISS astronaut, doctor discusses upcoming mission
summary: A NASA astronaut and doctor, experienced in mass casualty events and overseas service, discusses their upcoming mission to the International Space Station (ISS). The mission highlights NASA's continued focus on human spaceflight and scientific research in orbit.

title: SpaceX launches Falcon Heavy on Wednesday after 18 month hiatus
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its Falcon Heavy rocket after an 18-month hiatus. This marks a significant return for the heavy-lift vehicle.

title: Artemis III core stage assembly arrives at the Kennedy Space Center
summary: The core stage assembly for NASA's Artemis III mission has arrived at the Kennedy Space Center. This follows the successful Artemis II launch, which sent four astronauts on a lunar flyby mission.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon Heavy returns, Atlas V and Ariane 6 to launch Amazon Leo satellites
summary: Falcon Heavy, Atlas V, and Ariane 6 are set to launch Amazon's Leo satellites this week from multiple locations. SpaceX's Falcon Heavy is making a return for these missions.

title: Curiosity Rover uncovers fresh organic clues on Ancient Mars
summary: NASA's Curiosity Rover has discovered new organic clues on Mars, advancing the search for ancient life beyond Earth. The findings were reported by NASASpaceFlight.com.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on a recent development in the space industry. No specific details about the news content were provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 significant achievements over the past 50 years. This commemorative publication celebrates the agency's milestones in space exploration and technology.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space mission is undergoing a critical parachute testing phase, involving high-temperature baking to ensure reliability during re-entry. This process is essential for validating the parachute system's performance under extreme conditions.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination of Mars. The parachute, made of nylon and Kevlar, will slow the rover during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing objects in space. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and debris removal.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, remaining on track for a 2027 launch to search for terrestrial planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual SME Forum 2026, part of SME and Midcap Days held on 8-9 June 2026 at ESA Headquarters in Paris. The event includes an online preparatory forum in April and May.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: The article discusses the successful transmission of Celeste's first navigation signal. It highlights the significance of this milestone for the space industry.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has conducted 57 artificial solar eclipses since July 2025, collecting over 250 hours of high-resolution solar corona data. The mission revealed that solar wind structures in the inner corona travel 3-4 times faster than previously believed.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, scheduled for 16–17 September at the agency's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Overview Energy Wins Air Force Contract
summary: Overview Energy has won an Air Force contract to provide continuous power to military bases using space-based solar energy. The company aims to mitigate fuel line vulnerabilities by harnessing solar power day and night.

title: Astranis Raises $450M+, Including $300M Series E
summary: Astranis has raised over $450 million, including a $300 million Series E funding round led by Snowpoint Ventures and Franklin Templeton. The company's valuation has reached $2.8 billion according to a source close to the deal.

title: Scout Space Closes $18M Series A
summary: Scout Space has secured $18 million in Series A funding. The company plans to expand by building a new satellite sensor factory near Washington, D.C., and nearly doubling its workforce within 18 months.

title: LiveEO Raises €28M+ to Fund its First Constellation
summary: LiveEO, a German company, has raised over €28 million to fund its first satellite constellation. The funds will be used to scale operations, launch the constellation, and develop technology to meet increasing demand for Earth observation data.

title: Commerce Aiming to Open Novel Space Applications This Summer
summary: The Office of Space Commerce (OSC) plans to streamline licensing for novel space missions by serving as a centralized hub, with implementation expected this summer. This initiative aims to simplify regulatory processes for emerging space applications.

title: Bridenstine Takes Over as Quantum Space CEO
summary: Jim Bridenstine, former NASA administrator, has been appointed CEO of Quantum Space, returning to his background in national security and defense. The move marks a shift from his previous role at NASA.

title: Anduril Names Its SBI Team For Golden Dome
summary: Anduril has named its team for the Space-Based Interceptor (SBI) component of the Golden Dome missile defense architecture. The company is one of 12 prime contractors involved in this project.

title: ESA Taps Edge Aerospace for Space Cloud Contract
summary: ESA has selected Edge Aerospace, a Luxembourg-based company, to develop an architecture and roadmap for orbital data centers under a new space cloud contract. This initiative aims to advance in-space data processing capabilities.

title: Lt. Gen. Schiess Nominated to Lead Space Force
summary: Lt. Gen. Schiess has been nominated to lead the U.S. Space Force. If confirmed, he will become the third chief of space operations since the Space Force's establishment in 2019.

title: Payload Field Guide: Lunar Rovers
summary: NASA and its partners are advancing lunar rover technology for sustained human presence on the Moon, moving beyond Apollo-era designs to support long-term surface operations. Modern lunar rovers are expected to handle significant payload capacity and operational demands.

title: Enabled by AI, NGA Director Focused on Mission, Transformation, and Workforce
summary: The National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency (NGA) is leveraging AI to enhance its mission, transformation, and workforce to address evolving threats and warfighter needs. The agency's new director is prioritizing resilience and the adoption of emerging technologies.

title: Telespazio to Distribute Greenerwave Multi-Orbit Terminals to European Defense Market
summary: Telespazio France will distribute multi-orbit terminals developed by Greenerwave to the European defense market under a new agreement. The collaboration aims to enhance satellite communication capabilities for defense applications.

title: Verizon Details ‘Satellite-Everywhere’ for Disaster Response, Expands Satellite Fleet
summary: Verizon is expanding its satellite fleet to enhance disaster response capabilities, aiming for a 'satellite-everywhere' failover strategy. This move supports its broader goal of improving connectivity during emergencies.

title: Astranis Raises $300M in Series E and Additional Access to Capital
summary: Astranis, a satellite manufacturer, raised $300 million in a Series E funding round and secured up to $155 million in additional capital to boost satellite production. The funds will accelerate the company's efforts to expand its satellite manufacturing capabilities.

title: SWISSto12 Strikes German Partnership Deal for D2D GEO Satellite
summary: SWISSto12 has formed a new partnership deal in Germany to enhance media broadcast capabilities for Geostationary (GEO) satellites. The collaboration aims to develop Direct-to-Device (D2D) satellite technology.

title: Anduril to Work With Impulse Space, K2 Space, Voyager, and Others on Space-Based Interceptor Award
summary: Anduril Industries has formed a team of collaborators, including Impulse Space, K2 Space, and others, to prototype space-based interceptor technologies. The collaboration follows Anduril's recent award for this initiative.

title: Telesat’s GEO Business Declines 25% in Q1
summary: Telesat's GEO business revenue declined by 25% year-over-year in Q1 2026 as the company shifts focus toward launching its Lightspeed constellation. CEO Dan Goldberg attributed the decline to this strategic transition.

title: What Amazon’s Globalstar Acquisition Means for MSS Spectrum and D2D Market: Analyst Roundtable
summary: Amazon acquired Globalstar in a $10.8 billion deal, signaling its expansion into the Mobile Satellite Services (MSS) spectrum and direct-to-device (D2D) market. Analysts discuss the implications of this acquisition for the space industry.

title: NRO Makes New Commercial Awards to EarthDaily, Iceye, and Pixxel
summary: The National Reconnaissance Office (NRO) awarded new contracts for Earth sensing data to EarthDaily, Iceye, and Pixxel under its Strategic Commercial Enhancements (SCE) effort. This expands the NRO's commercial satellite data partnerships.

title: STMicroelectronics Expects its Space Business to Reach More than $3B Over Three Years
summary: STMicroelectronics, a semiconductor company supplying SpaceX and others, expects over $3 billion in space business revenue over the next three years.
